problem: Let $P(x)$ be a polynomial such that when $P(x)$ is divided by $x - 19,$ the remainder is 99, and when $P(x)$ is divided by $x - 99,$ the remainder is 19.  What is the remainder when $P(x)$ is divided by $(x - 19)(x - 99)$?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain what the Remainder Theorem is and how it applies to this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the form of the remainder when $P(x)$ is divided by $(x-19)(x-99)$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What can we infer about the degree of the remainder in this case?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we use the fact that $P(19)=99$ and $P(99)=19$ to solve for the coefficients of the remainder?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the coefficient of $x$ in the remainder must be $-1$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use the coefficients we solved for to write the final expression for the remainder?
classified_questions.label: b
